paper_title: Experimental Methods for Studying Diatom Electrical Activity

methods.method_name: Transducer Setup and Electrical Noise Measurement
methods.description: This method describes the setup of a transducer for measuring electrical noise in diatom suspensions and the conditions under which these measurements are taken.

steps.description: The transducer consists of a glass substrate on which circular electrodes of 100 nm Au on top of a 4 nm Cr adhesion layer were evaporated through a shadow mask.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare glass substrate with Au and Cr electrodes

steps.description: A drilled PMMA well was glued on top of the substrate with the prefabricated electrodes.
An aliquot of 1 ml of a diatom cell suspension in standard Guillard's (F/2) Marine Water Enrichment medium with a concentration of 1.9 × 10^5 cells/ml was transferred into the well.
The cells were allowed to settle on to the electrodes for 2 h before any measurements were performed.
steps.searchable_summary: allow diatoms to settle on electrodes

steps.description: Current noise measurements were performed by a Stanford low-noise current amplifier (SRS 570).
External interference was minimized through the use of a Faraday cage and low noise cables.

methods.method_name: Pseudo-nitzschia fraudulenta Culture and Cell Counting
methods.description: This method outlines the culturing conditions and cell counting procedures for Pseudo-nitzschia fraudulenta diatoms.

steps.description: Experiments were carried out using a clonal non-axenic strain of Pseudo-nitzschia fraudulenta.
steps.searchable_summary: use clonal strain of Pseudo-nitzschia fraudulenta

steps.description: A batch culture was initiated for biomass growth in 1 l borosilicate Erlenmeyer flask at 19 °C, 33-34 PSU, illuminated with cool-white fluorescent lights with a flux of 120 μmol.m−2.s−1 on a light-dark cycle of 12 hours each.
steps.searchable_summary: initiate batch culture for diatom growth

steps.description: The culture was gently homogenized before 3 ml samples were taken from each flask in triplicate, daily, for cell count.
steps.searchable_summary: take daily samples for cell count

steps.description: Cells were preserved with Lugol's iodine solution and counted using an inverted microscope (Leica DMi8) at 100x magnification.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve and count cells using microscope

methods.description: This method describes the procedure for conducting a cell proliferation assay using the CellTiter 96 AQueous One Solution.

steps.description: Assays were carried out in 96-well plates using the CellTiter 96 AQueous One Solution Cell Proliferation Assay according to the manufacturer's instructions.
Cells were plated in quadruplicate wells containing 100 ml of medium and GdCl3.
steps.searchable_summary: plate cells in wells with medium and GdCl3

steps.description: The concentrations used in the viability experiments varied from 10 to 1000 µM for GdCl3 and cell proliferation was evaluated after 24 hours.

methods.method_name: Fluorescence Tracking of Intracellular Calcium Localization
methods.description: This method involves staining diatom cells to track intracellular calcium localization using fluorescence microscopy.

steps.description: Pseudo-nitzschia fraudulenta cells were harvested, placed in a µ-dish (Ibidi) and stained for 60 minutes with Oregon Green BAPTA-1 AM (Thermo Fisher) at 10 µM with a final concentration of 0.1% DMSO.
steps.searchable_summary: stain cells with Oregon Green BAPTA-1 AM

steps.description: Observed immediately using a DeltaVision System. Autofluorescence was acquired in the red channel under blue excitation and Oregon Green was acquired in the GFP channel.
steps.searchable_summary: observe stained cells using fluorescence microscopy

methods.description: This method describes the use of microscopy for image acquisition and post-processing of diatom cells.

steps.description: Image acquisition was performed using a Nikon Eclipse TE2000-S equipped with a Hamamatsu Flash 2.8 sCMOS 2.8Mpx and micromanager software for controlling the microscope and camera.
steps.searchable_summary: acquire images using Nikon Eclipse TE2000-S

steps.description: ImageJ was then used for post-processing operations such as, measuring and counting cells.
